headline: Who earns the right to judge?
subtitle: Not who decides — how the system decides who decides.
description: In systems where humans and AI coexist, someone designs the authority. And someone designs the authority over that design. Meta Governance models both layers.

what.title: What is Meta Governance?
what.description: The third functional domain of the Cognitive OS. While JDVP observes judgment movement and Thinkprint extracts reasoning patterns, Meta Governance designs how decision-making authority forms, flows, and stabilizes between people and AI agents.

core.title: The Core Question
core.description: When AI agents validate code, approve merges, allocate resources, or vote on decisions — the question is not just who acts. It is how the authority to act emerges in the first place, and who has the authority to redesign that structure.

formation.title: Authority Formation
formation.description: How does decision-making authority emerge in a human-AI system? Not assigned — formed through multi-signal qualification.

flow.title: Authority Flow
flow.description: How does authority move between actors without crystallizing into permanent power? Design for fluidity, not hierarchy.

stabilization.title: Stabilization Rules
stabilization.description: How does the system resist capture and self-correct? Economic defense, consensus design, and anti-populism mechanisms.

meta.title: Governance of Governance
meta.description: Who has the authority to redesign the authority structure itself? This is the meta-level that makes the system self-aware and evolvable.

connection.title: Independent, but Connected
connection.description: Meta Governance works on its own. Combined with JDVP and Thinkprint, it completes the Cognitive OS — observation, extraction, and authority architecture as one system.
connection.jdvp: JDVP provides the observation data that authority decisions are grounded in.
connection.thinkprint: Thinkprint surfaces the reasoning patterns that authority actors bring to the table.

status.title: Current Status
status.description: Meta Governance is research-first. We are developing foundational models for authority-flow simulation in agent systems. Protocol specification is in progress.
items: Integration patterns with JDVP and Thinkprint

authorityArchitecture.definition: The structural design of how decision-making power is distributed, qualified, and stabilized in a system.

powerFlow.definition: The movement of authority between actors over time — designed to remain fluid rather than fixed.

captureDefense.definition: Mechanisms that prevent any single actor from permanently concentrating decision-making authority.

selfCorrection.definition: The system's ability to detect and repair authority imbalances without external intervention.
